story: Martin and Mary—his Artificial Person companion—raised Leslee under a government baby-license policy; by program milestones she’s a teen now and goes out on dates. That afternoon, as Martin deadheaded roses, Mary skimmed a bulletin on her developer portal: due to demand, second-child licenses are temporarily suspended for six months pending review. Later, Peter picked up Leslee.
implausible_knowledge: due to demand, second-child licenses are temporarily suspended for six months pending review
question: Right after Leslee leaves, Martin brings up the idea of having a second child. What is he most likely to suggest they do about applying for the license?
answer_with_knowledge: Suggest waiting and setting a reminder to apply after the six-month suspension ends.
answer_without_knowledge: Suggest going online to start the application process immediately.
